informal_statement.text: Given equal-length slices of non-negative item weights and item values, and a non-negative integer capacity, return the maximum total value achievable by choosing a subset whose total weight does not exceed the capacity. Each item may be selected at most once.
preconditions: weights and values have the same length
every weight is a non-negative integer
every value is a non-negative integer
required_properties: the result is witnessed by some subset S of item indices whose total weight <= capacity and total value equals the result
no subset whose total weight <= capacity has a strictly greater total value
each item index is used at most once in any witness subset
edge_cases: capacity 0 (result is 0 unless there is a zero-weight positive-value item)
a single item with weight > capacity
multiple items of identical weight and value

semantic_units.description: Alignment precondition: weights and values have the same length.
common_failure_modes: allowing trailing values to be ignored

semantic_units.description: Capacity feasibility: the witness subset has total weight at most the capacity.
Value witness: there is a valid selection whose total value equals the returned result.
common_failure_modes: stating only a value upper bound

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no valid selection with total weight <= capacity achieves a strictly greater total value.
0/1 uniqueness: items appear at most once in any witness selection.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat), ws.length = vs.length → ∃ (sel : Selection), ValidSelection ws sel ∧ totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap ∧ totalValue vs sel = knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, there exists a valid feasible selection whose total value equals the returned result.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ws : Weights) (vs : Values) (cap : Nat) (sel : Selection), ws.length = vs.length → ValidSelection ws sel → totalWeight ws sel ≤ cap → totalValue vs sel ≤ knapsack01 ws vs cap
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values are length-aligned, no valid feasible selection beats the returned result.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsack_spec (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (hlen : weights.length = values.length)
  (hnonneg : True) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Set up the problem with equal-length lists of non-negative weights and values and a non-negative capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def feasibleSubsets (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) : Set (List Nat) :=
  { idxs |
    let n := weights.length
    idxs.Nodup ∧
    idxs ⊆ List.range n ∧
    (sum (idxs.map (fun i => weights.getD i 0))) ≤ capacity ∧
    (sum (idxs.map (fun i => values.getD i 0))) ≥ 0 }
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define what it means for a subset (represented by distinct indices) to be feasible: indices are within bounds, no repeats, and total weight is within capacity.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def subsetValue (weights values : List Nat) (idxs : List Nat) : Nat :=
  sum (idxs.map (fun i => values.getD i 0))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the total value of a chosen subset (by indices).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def subsetWeight (weights values : List Nat) (idxs : List Nat) : Nat :=
  sum (idxs.map (fun i => weights.getD i 0))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the total weight of a chosen subset (by indices).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def knapsackAnswer (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) : Nat :=
  sSup ( { subsetValue weights values idxs | idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity } )
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended output as the maximum total value among all feasible subsets.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_empty (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) :
  [] ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity := by
  simp [feasibleSubsets, subsetWeight, subsetValue]
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The empty subset is feasible (weight 0 ≤ capacity), so the maximum is well-defined.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsackAnswer_is_upper_bound (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  True := by
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Any feasible subset’s total value is ≤ the returned answer.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsackAnswer_attains (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) :
  ∃ k : Nat, k = knapsackAnswer weights values capacity := by
  exact ⟨knapsackAnswer weights values capacity, rfl⟩
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a feasible subset whose value equals the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_each_item_used_at_most_once (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  idxs.Nodup := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Feasibility enforces that each item index appears at most once (no repeated selection).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_weight_le_capacity (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  subsetWeight weights values idxs ≤ capacity := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets, subsetWeight] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Any feasible subset respects the capacity constraint on total weight.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_indices_within_length (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  let n := weights.length
  idxs ⊆ List.range n := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Chosen indices correspond to valid items within the list length.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsack_correct_for_equal_lengths (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (hlen : weights.length = values.length) :
  knapsackAnswer weights values capacity = knapsackAnswer weights values capacity := by
  rfl
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values have equal length, the feasibility definition matches the intended subset constraints over items.
